speaker: ADRIANA
text: Neither my husband nor the slave returned

speaker: ADRIANA
text: That in such haste I sent to seek his master?

speaker: ADRIANA
text: Sure, Luciana, it is two o’clock.

speaker: LUCIANA
text: Perhaps some merchant hath invited him,

speaker: LUCIANA
text: And from the mart he’s somewhere gone to dinner.

speaker: LUCIANA
text: Good sister, let us dine, and never fret.

speaker: LUCIANA
text: A man is master of his liberty;

speaker: LUCIANA
text: Time is their master, and when they see time

speaker: LUCIANA
text: They’ll go or come. If so, be patient, sister.

speaker: ADRIANA
text: Why should their liberty than ours be more?

speaker: LUCIANA
text: Because their business still lies out o’ door.

speaker: ADRIANA
text: Look when I serve him so, he takes it ill.

speaker: LUCIANA
text: O, know he is the bridle of your will.

speaker: ADRIANA
text: There’s none but asses will be bridled so.

speaker: LUCIANA
text: Why, headstrong liberty is lashed with woe.

speaker: LUCIANA
text: There’s nothing situate under heaven’s eye

speaker: LUCIANA
text: But hath his bound in earth, in sea, in sky.

speaker: LUCIANA
text: The beasts, the fishes, and the wingèd fowls

speaker: LUCIANA
text: Are their males’ subjects and at their controls.

speaker: LUCIANA
text: Man, more divine, the master of all these,

speaker: LUCIANA
text: Lord of the wide world and wild wat’ry seas,

speaker: LUCIANA
text: Endued with intellectual sense and souls,

speaker: LUCIANA
text: Of more preeminence than fish and fowls,

speaker: LUCIANA
text: Are masters to their females, and their lords.

speaker: LUCIANA
text: Then let your will attend on their accords.

speaker: ADRIANA
text: This servitude makes you to keep unwed.

speaker: LUCIANA
text: Not this, but troubles of the marriage bed.

speaker: ADRIANA
text: But, were you wedded, you would bear some sway.

speaker: LUCIANA
text: Ere I learn love, I’ll practice to obey.

speaker: ADRIANA
text: How if your husband start some otherwhere?

speaker: LUCIANA
text: Till he come home again, I would forbear.

speaker: ADRIANA
text: Patience unmoved! No marvel though she pause;

speaker: ADRIANA
text: They can be meek that have no other cause.

speaker: ADRIANA
text: A wretched soul bruised with adversity

speaker: ADRIANA
text: We bid be quiet when we hear it cry,

speaker: ADRIANA
text: But were we burdened with like weight of pain,

speaker: ADRIANA
text: As much or more we should ourselves complain.

speaker: ADRIANA
text: So thou, that hast no unkind mate to grieve thee,

speaker: ADRIANA
text: With urging helpless patience would relieve me;

speaker: ADRIANA
text: But if thou live to see like right bereft,

speaker: ADRIANA
text: This fool-begged patience in thee will be left.

speaker: LUCIANA
text: Well, I will marry one day, but to try.

speaker: LUCIANA
text: Here comes your man. Now is your husband nigh.

speaker: ADRIANA
text: Say, is your tardy master now at hand?

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: Nay, he’s at two hands with me,

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: and that my two ears can witness.

speaker: ADRIANA
text: Say, didst thou speak with him? Know’st thou his mind?

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: Ay, ay, he told his mind upon mine ear.

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: Beshrew his hand, I scarce could understand it.

speaker: LUCIANA
text: Spake he so doubtfully thou couldst not feel

speaker: LUCIANA
text: his meaning?

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: Nay, he struck so plainly I could

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: too well feel his blows, and withal so doubtfully

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: that I could scarce understand them.

speaker: ADRIANA
text: But say, I prithee, is he coming home?

speaker: ADRIANA
text: It seems he hath great care to please his wife.

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: Why, mistress, sure my master is horn mad.

speaker: ADRIANA
text: Horn mad, thou villain?

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: I mean not cuckold mad,

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: But sure he is stark mad.

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: When I desired him to come home to dinner,

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: He asked me for a thousand marks in gold.

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: “’Tis dinnertime,” quoth I. “My gold,” quoth he.

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: “Your meat doth burn,” quoth I. “My gold,” quoth he.

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: “Will you come?” quoth I. “My gold,” quoth he.

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: “Where is the thousand marks I gave thee, villain?”

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: “The pig,” quoth I, “is burned.” “My gold,” quoth he.

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: “My mistress, sir,” quoth I. “Hang up thy mistress!

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: I know not thy mistress. Out on thy mistress!”

speaker: LUCIANA
text: Quoth who?

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: Quoth my master.

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: “I know,” quoth he, “no house, no wife, no mistress.”

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: So that my errand, due unto my tongue,

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: I thank him, I bare home upon my shoulders,

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: For, in conclusion, he did beat me there.

speaker: ADRIANA
text: Go back again, thou slave, and fetch him home.

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: Go back again and be new beaten home?

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: For God’s sake, send some other messenger.

speaker: ADRIANA
text: Back, slave, or I will break thy pate across.

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: And he will bless that cross with other beating.

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: Between you, I shall have a holy head.

speaker: ADRIANA
text: Hence, prating peasant. Fetch thy master home.

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: Am I so round with you as you with me,

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: That like a football you do spurn me thus?

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: You spurn me hence, and he will spurn me hither.

speaker:
DROMIO
               
              OF
               
              EPHESUS
text: If I last in this service, you must case me in leather.

speaker: LUCIANA
text: Fie, how impatience loureth in your face.

speaker: ADRIANA
text: His company must do his minions grace,

speaker: ADRIANA
text: Whilst I at home starve for a merry look.

speaker: ADRIANA
text: Hath homely age th’ alluring beauty took

speaker: ADRIANA
text: From my poor cheek? Then he hath wasted it.

speaker: ADRIANA
text: Are my discourses dull? Barren my wit?

speaker: ADRIANA
text: If voluble and sharp discourse be marred,

speaker: ADRIANA
text: Unkindness blunts it more than marble hard.

speaker: ADRIANA
text: Do their gay vestments his affections bait?

speaker: ADRIANA
text: That’s not my fault; he’s master of my state.

speaker: ADRIANA
text: What ruins are in me that can be found

speaker: ADRIANA
text: By him not ruined? Then is he the ground

speaker: ADRIANA
text: Of my defeatures. My decayèd fair

speaker: ADRIANA
text: A sunny look of his would soon repair.

speaker: ADRIANA
text: But, too unruly deer, he breaks the pale

speaker: ADRIANA
text: And feeds from home. Poor I am but his stale.

speaker: LUCIANA
text: Self-harming jealousy, fie, beat it hence.

speaker: ADRIANA
text: Unfeeling fools can with such wrongs dispense.

speaker: ADRIANA
text: I know his eye doth homage otherwhere,

speaker: ADRIANA
text: Or else what lets it but he would be here?

speaker: ADRIANA
text: Sister, you know he promised me a chain.

speaker: ADRIANA
text: Would that alone o’ love he would detain,

speaker: ADRIANA
text: So he would keep fair quarter with his bed.

speaker: ADRIANA
text: I see the jewel best enamelèd

speaker: ADRIANA
text: Will lose his beauty. Yet the gold bides still

speaker: ADRIANA
text: That others touch, and often touching will

speaker: ADRIANA
text: Wear gold; yet no man that hath a name

speaker: ADRIANA
text: By falsehood and corruption doth it shame.

speaker: ADRIANA
text: Since that my beauty cannot please his eye,

speaker: ADRIANA
text: I’ll weep what’s left away, and weeping die.

speaker: LUCIANA
text: How many fond fools serve mad jealousy!
